title: Explicit heat-equation update is L-infinity stable under the parabolic CFL condition

source.citation: Randall J. LeVeque, Finite Difference Methods for Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations, SIAM, 2007, Ch. 9.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If 0 <= r and 2r <= 1, and the left, center, and right scalar inputs are all bounded in absolute value by M, then the explicit heat update is also bounded by M.
informal.latex: If \(0\le r\), \(2r\le1\), \(|u_L|\le M\), \(|u|\le M\), and \(|u_R|\le M\), then \(|{\mathcal H}_r(u_L,u,u_R)|\le M\).
assumptions: r, uLeft, u, uRight, and M are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The scalar explicit heat update remains bounded by M in absolute value.

declarations.name: heatStep
declarations.statement: def heatStep (r uLeft u uRight : Real) : Real := heatLeftWeight r * uLeft + heatCenterWeight r * u + heatRightWeight r * uRight

declarations.name: heatStep_abs_le
declarations.statement: theorem heatStep_abs_le (r uLeft u uRight M : Real) (hr0 : 0 <= r) (hrCfl : 2 * r <= 1) (hLeft : |uLeft| <= M) (h : |u| <= M) (hRight : |uRight| <= M) : |heatStep r uLeft u uRight| <= M

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: If 0 <= r and 2r <= 1, and the left, center, and right scalar inputs are all bounded in absolute value by M, then the explicit heat update is also bounded by M.
tasks.target: theorem heatStep_abs_le (r uLeft u uRight M : Real) (hr0 : 0 <= r) (hrCfl : 2 * r <= 1) (hLeft : |uLeft| <= M) (h : |u| <= M) (hRight : |uRight| <= M) : |heatStep r uLeft u uRight| <= M

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using heatWeights_nonneg, repeated abs_add_le, abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg, mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left, linarith, and ring.

tasks.input: Find core Mathlib lemmas needed to prove L-infinity stability of a three-term convex combination.
